events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need parse scenario. Task: identify component or condition shown, name visible defect cues. Setting heavy industrial I&C loop checks & wiring. Observed field condition: wire labels vs. loop drawing mismatch (DOC compare). Work rough-complete. Condition categories may apply installed-defective. Need decision fail because non-compliant mismatch. Risk? Wire labels mismatch can cause incorrect termination, commissioning errors, safety instrumented function mis-operation, process hazards. In heavy industrial, high risk maybe critical? Let's assess. Mismatch between field wire labels and loop drawings means as-built not per design. Could lead to wrong loop, failed loop check, potential safety issue. At rough-complete, not energized maybe. But documentation error is non-compliance. Risk high. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete rough-complete maybe 75. Value not asked 0. Sound etc default. Findings: wire labels do not match loop drawing; incorrect or missing labeling; as-built condition deviates from engineered documentation. Actions: verify all terminations against loop drawings; replace/correct wire labels; re-check continuity and loop function; update as-builts; re-inspect. Rationale: ISA 5.1 and loop drawings require correct identification of wires; mismatch is a defect and non-compliance. References: ISA 5.1, loop drawings, project specs/permits. Need JSON valid. Ensure no extra prose.

    "Installed wiring identification is incorrect or inconsistent with engineered documentation.",

  "actions": [
    "Stop loop check and verify every wire termination against the approved loop drawing.",
    "Remove or cover incorrect labels and apply correct, durable wire labels per project spec and ISA 5.1.",
    "Perform continuity and point-to-point checks after re-labeling.",
    "Update as-built drawings and loop documentation to reflect actual field conditions.",
    "Re-inspect and sign off the loop check before energizing or commissioning."
  ],
  "rationale": "Wire labels must match the loop drawing so terminations, signals, and safety instrumented functions can be verified correctly. A label-to-drawing mismatch is a non-compliant, defective installation that invalidates the loop check and creates risk of mis-wiring during commissioning.",

    "ISA 5.1 - Instrumentation Symbols and Identification",
    "Approved loop drawings",
    "Project specifications and permits"
